brief_title: A Phase 1 Study of MEDI4736 in Combination With Tremelimumab in Subjects With Advanced Solid Tumors
official_title: A Phase I Study of MEDI4736 (Anti-PD-L1 Antibody) in Combination With Tremelimumab (Anti-CTLA-4 Antibody) in Subjects With Advanced Solid Tumors
brief_summary: This is a multicenter, open-label, dose-exploration and dose-expansion study to evaluate the safety, tolerability, antitumor activity, PK and immunogenicity of MEDI4736 in combination with tremelimumab in subjects with select advanced solid tumors.

interventions.name: MEDI4736
interventions.description: MEDI4736 is administered by IV infusion in combination with tremelimumab

interventions.name: tremelimumab
interventions.description: Tremelimumab is administered by IV infusion in combination with MEDI4736

eligibility.criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Male and female subjects
* 18 years and older
* Histologic confirmation of advanced solid tumors
* Subjects must have recurrent/metastatic disease and may have been previously treated in the recurrent/metastatic setting.

Exclusion Criteria:

* Any concurrent chemotherapy, immune-mediated therapy or biologic or hormonal therapy for cancer treatment
* Active or prior documented autoimmune disease within the past 2 years
* Current or prior use of immunosuppressive medication within 14 days with some exceptions.

primary_outcomes.measure: Number of subjects reporting adverse events
primary_outcomes.timeFrame: Screening through 15 months after the last subject enters re-treatment

primary_outcomes.measure: Number of subjects reporting serious adverse events
primary_outcomes.timeFrame: Screening through 15 months after the last subject enters re-treatment

primary_outcomes.measure: Number of subjects experiencing a dose-limiting toxicity
primary_outcomes.timeFrame: First dose of study medication through 4 weeks after the first dose of study medication

primary_outcomes.measure: Change from Baseline in laboratory evaluations
primary_outcomes.timeFrame: Screening through 3 months after the last dose of study medication

primary_outcomes.measure: Change from Baseline in vital signs
primary_outcomes.timeFrame: Screening through 3 months after the last dose of study medication

primary_outcomes.measure: Change from Baseline in electrocardiogram evaluations
primary_outcomes.timeFrame: Screening through 3 months after the last dose of study medication

primary_outcomes.measure: Overall Response Rate in Select Tumor Types
primary_outcomes.timeFrame: First dose of study medication through 15 months after the last subject enters re-treatment
